audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + dus + clause]] and [[grammar:even + imperative/request]]. Use [[grammar:dus]] when the second clause follows logically from the first. Use [[grammar:even]] in a friendly request for a quick or small action.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:clause + dus + clause]]: [[grammar:Dus]] coordinates a consequential relation and normally retains main-clause word order.; [[grammar:even + imperative/request]]: The modal particle reduces imposition without changing the core propositional content.

audienceSections.normal:
#### clause + dus + clause

Use [[grammar:dus]] when the second clause follows logically from the first. Example: [[grammar:Het boek is niet beschikbaar, dus ik reserveer het.]]

#### even + imperative/request

Use [[grammar:even]] in a friendly request for a quick or small action. Example: [[grammar:Kijk even in de catalogus.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + dus + clause

[[grammar:Dus]] coordinates a consequential relation and normally retains main-clause word order. Literal example: [[grammar:Het boek is niet beschikbaar, dus ik reserveer het.]]

#### even + imperative/request

The modal particle reduces imposition without changing the core propositional content. Literal example: [[grammar:Kijk even in de catalogus.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Dus]] introduces a consequence. [[grammar:Even]] can soften a short request or instruction.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Dus]] introduces a consequence. [[grammar:Even]] can soften a short request or instruction. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Ik zoek een boek over fotografie.]] — I am looking for a book about photography.
- [[grammar:Kijk even in de catalogus.]] — Take a quick look in the catalogue.
- [[grammar:Het boek staat daar niet, dus ik ga naar de balie.]] — The book is not listed there, so I am going to the counter.
- [[grammar:Kijk even of een andere bibliotheek het heeft.]] — Check whether another library has it.
- [[grammar:Het boek is niet beschikbaar, dus ik reserveer het.]] — The book is unavailable, so I reserve it.
- [[grammar:Wanneer is het weer beschikbaar?]] — When is it available again?
- [[grammar:Volgende week kan ik het lenen.]] — Next week I can borrow it.
- [[grammar:Schrijf even je telefoonnummer op.]] — Write down your phone number for a moment.
- [[grammar:Goed, dan krijg ik een bericht.]] — Good, then I will receive a message.
- [[grammar:De catalogus is handig, maar niet altijd compleet.]] — The catalogue is useful, but not always complete.
- [[grammar:Precies, dus controleer het ook bij de balie.]] — Exactly, so check it at the counter too.
- [[grammar:Dank u, ik regel het bij de balie.]] — Thank you, I will arrange it at the counter.
